info.title: Orang Command-Line Tool version
info.description: Search, replace, rename and delete directories, files and its content using the power of .NET regular expressions.

commands.name: copy
commands.description: Searches the file system for files and directories and copy them to a destination directory.

options.name: --after-context
options.description: Number of lines to show after matching line.

options.name: --align-columns
options.description: Align columns.

options.name: --ask
options.description: Ask for a permission to copy file or directory.

options.name: --attributes
options.description: File attributes that are required.

options.name: --attributes-to-skip
options.description: File attributes that should be skipped.

options.name: --before-context
options.description: Number of lines to show before matching line.

options.name: --compare
options.description: File properties to be compared.

options.name: --conflict
options.description: Defines how to resolve conflict when a file/directory already exists.

options.name: --content
options.description: Regular expression for files' content.

options.name: --content-mode
options.description: Defines which parts of a content should be included in the results.

options.name: --context
options.description: Number of lines to show before and after matching line.

options.name: --count
options.description: Show number of matches in a file.

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Display which files/directories should be copied but do not actually copy any file/directory.

options.name: --encoding
options.description: Encoding to use when a file does not contain BOM. Default encoding is UTF-8.

options.name: --exclude
options.description: Space separated list of glob patterns to exclude files and/or folders.

options.name: --extension
options.description: A filter for file extensions (case-insensitive by default).

options.name: --flat
options.description: Copy files directly into target directory.

options.name: --highlight
options.description: Parts of the output to highlight.

options.name: --include
options.description: Space separated list of glob patterns to include files and/or folders.

options.name: --include-directory
options.description: [deprecated] Regular expression for a directory name.

options.name: --line-number
options.description: Include line number.

options.name: --max-count
options.description: Stop searching after specified number is reached.

options.name: --max-depth
options.description: Maximum directory depth.

options.name: --max-matches-in-file
options.description: Stop searching in a file after specified number of matches is found.

options.name: --max-matching-files
options.description: Stop searching after specified number of files is found.

options.name: --name
options.description: Regular expression for file or directory name.

options.name: --no-content
options.description: A shortcut for '--content-mode omit'.

options.name: --no-path
options.description: A shortcut for '--path-mode omit'.

options.name: --no-recurse
options.description: Do not search subdirectories.

options.name: --output
options.description: Path to a file that should store output.

options.name: --path-mode
options.description: Defines which part of a path should be included in the results.

options.name: --paths
options.description: Path to one or more files and/or directories that should be searched.

options.name: --paths-from
options.description: Read the list of paths to search from a file. Paths should be separated by newlines.

options.name: --progress
options.description: Display dot (.) for every hundredth searched file or directory.

options.name: --properties
options.description: Display file's properties and optionally filter by that properties.

options.name: --sort
options.description: Sort matched files and directories.

options.name: --summary
options.description: Show summary at the end of search.

options.name: --target
options.description: A directory to copy files and directories to. It can be also specified as a last unnamed parameter.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The amount of information to display in the log.

arguments.name: <PATH>
arguments.description: Path to one or more files and/or directories that should be searched.

arguments.name: <PATH>
arguments.description: Path to one or more source directories and optionally a target directory.

options.name: --ends-with
options.description: Pattern should match from the end of the input string.

options.name: --equals
options.description: Pattern should match whole input string.

options.name: --explicit-capture
options.description: Do not capture unnamed groups.

options.name: --from-file
options.description: Interpret argument value as path to a file whose content will be used as an input.

options.name: --ignore-case
options.description: Use case-insensitive matching.

options.name: --ignore-pattern-whitespace
options.description: Exclude unescaped white-space from the pattern and enable comments after '#' sign.

options.name: --list
options.description: Interpret pattern as a list of pattern any of which has to be matched. Separator is either comma or newline if the patter is loaded from a file.

options.name: --literal
options.description: Text should be treated as a literal expression not as a regular expression.

options.name: --multiline
options.description: ^ and $ match beginning and end of each line (instead of beginning and end  of the input string).

options.name: --separator
options.description: String that separates each value in a list. Default value is comma or newline if the list is loaded from a file.

options.name: --singleline
options.description: Period matches every character (instead of every character except \n.

options.name: --starts-with
options.description: Pattern should match from the beginning of the input string.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The amount of information to display in the log.

options.name: --whole-line
options.description: Pattern should match whole line.

options.name: --whole-word
options.description: Pattern should match whole word.

commands.name: regex-escape
commands.description: Escapes special characters by replacing them with their escape codes.

options.name: --char-group
options.description: Text is part of a character group.

options.name: --input
options.description: Text to be escaped.

options.name: --output
options.description: Path to a file that should store output.

options.name: --replacement
options.description: Text is a replacement string.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The amount of information to display in the log.

options.name: --char-group
options.description: Treat character as if it is in the character group.

options.name: --filter
options.description: Regular expression to filter patterns (case-insensitive by default).

options.name: --options
options.description: Regex options that should be used. Relevant values are ecma-script or ignore-case.

options.name: --section
options.description: Syntax sections to filter.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The amount of information to display in the log.

arguments.name: <CHAR>
arguments.description: Character or a decimal number that represents the character. For a number literal use escape like \1.

options.description: The input string to be searched.

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Enable editing of a replacement.

options.name: --line-number
options.description: Include line number.

options.name: --max-count
options.description: Stop searching after specified number is reached.

options.name: --max-depth
options.description: Maximum directory depth.

options.name: --max-matches-in-file
options.description: Stop searching in a file after specified number of matches is found.

options.name: --max-matching-files
options.description: Stop searching after specified number of files is found.

options.name: --max-word-length
options.description: Specifies maximal word length to be checked.

options.name: --min-word-length
options.description: Specifies minimal word length to be checked. Default value is 3.

options.name: --name
options.description: Regular expression for file or directory name.

options.name: --no-content
options.description: A shortcut for '--content-mode omit'.

options.name: --no-path
options.description: A shortcut for '--path-mode omit'.

options.name: --no-recurse
options.description: Do not search subdirectories.

options.name: --output
options.description: Path to a file that should store output.

options.name: --path-mode
options.description: Defines which part of a path should be included in the results.

options.name: --paths
options.description: Path to one or more files and/or directories that should be searched.

options.name: --paths-from
options.description: Read the list of paths to search from a file. Paths should be separated by newlines.

options.name: --pipe
options.description: Defines how to use redirected/piped input.

options.name: --progress
options.description: Display dot (.) for every hundredth searched file or directory.

options.name: --properties
options.description: Display file's properties and optionally filter by that properties.

options.name: --sort
options.description: Sort matched files and directories.

options.name: --summary
options.description: Show summary at the end of search.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The amount of information to display in the log.

arguments.name: <PATH>
arguments.description: Path to one or more files and/or directories that should be searched.

options.name: --after-context
options.description: Number of lines to show after matching line.

options.name: --align-columns
options.description: Align columns.

options.name: --ask
options.description: Ask for a permission to synchronize file or directory.

options.name: --attributes
options.description: File attributes that are required.

options.name: --attributes-to-skip
options.description: File attributes that should be skipped.

options.name: --before-context
options.description: Number of lines to show before matching line.

options.name: --compare
options.description: File properties to be compared.

options.name: --conflict
options.description: Action to choose if a file or directory exists in one directory and it is missing in the second directory.

options.name: --content
options.description: Regular expression for files' content.

options.name: --content-mode
options.description: Defines which parts of a content should be included in the results.

options.name: --context
options.description: Number of lines to show before and after matching line.

options.name: --count
options.description: Show number of matches in a file.

options.name: --dry-run
options.description: Display which files or directories should be copied/deleted but do not actually copy/delete any file or directory.

options.name: --encoding
options.description: Encoding to use when a file does not contain BOM. Default encoding is UTF-8.

options.name: --exclude
options.description: Space separated list of glob patterns to exclude files and/or folders.

options.name: --extension
options.description: A filter for file extensions (case-insensitive by default).

options.name: --highlight
options.description: Parts of the output to highlight.

options.name: --include
options.description: Space separated list of glob patterns to include files and/or folders.

options.name: --include-directory
options.description: [deprecated] Regular expression for a directory name.

options.name: --line-number
options.description: Include line number.

options.name: --max-count
options.description: Stop searching after specified number is reached.

options.name: --max-depth
options.description: Maximum directory depth.

options.name: --max-matches-in-file
options.description: Stop searching in a file after specified number of matches is found.

options.name: --max-matching-files
options.description: Stop searching after specified number of files is found.

options.name: --name
options.description: Regular expression for file or directory name.

options.name: --no-content
options.description: A shortcut for '--content-mode omit'.

options.name: --no-path
options.description: A shortcut for '--path-mode omit'.

options.name: --no-recurse
options.description: Do not search subdirectories.

options.name: --output
options.description: Path to a file that should store output.

options.name: --path-mode
options.description: Defines which part of a path should be included in the results.

options.name: --paths
options.description: Path to one or more files and/or directories that should be searched.

options.name: --paths-from
options.description: Read the list of paths to search from a file. Paths should be separated by newlines.

options.name: --progress
options.description: Display dot (.) for every hundredth searched file or directory.

options.name: --properties
options.description: Display file's properties and optionally filter by that properties.

options.name: --second
options.description: A directory to be synchronized with a first directory. It can be also specified as a last unnamed parameter.

options.name: --sort
options.description: Sort matched files and directories.

options.name: --summary
options.description: Show summary at the end of search.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: The amount of information to display in the log.

arguments.description: Path to one or more files and/or directories that should be searched.

arguments.name: <PATH>
arguments.description: Path to a first directory to be synchronized and optionally a second directory.
